claims.text: On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General determined that the epidemic of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the DRC and Uganda constitutes a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).
As of 17 June 2026, the Democratic Republic of the Congo had reported 896 confirmed cases of Bundibugyo virus disease including 232 deaths.

As of 30 June 2026, the Democratic Republic of Congo reported 1,406 confirmed cases and 438 related deaths (208 recovered); Uganda reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths as of 1 July 2026 (15 recovered).

description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo declared its 17th Ebola outbreak — caused by Bundibugyo virus — on 15 May 2026, centred in Ituri Province. On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General declared the DRC and Uganda epidemic a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC). Confirmed cases and deaths have continued to climb through the end of June.

impact.summary: As of 30 June 2026 the DRC had reported 1,406 confirmed cases including 438 deaths; Uganda reported 20 confirmed cases and two deaths as of 1 July 2026, with 208 and 15 recoveries respectively.

location.region: Ituri Province (Mongbwalu), DRC, with cases in Uganda

name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
